cataloging_subject.label: Harley Lyrics (selections)
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The cataloged unit is the exact thirty-one-item edited selection of fresh Harley manuscript-facsimile transcriptions deposited as OTA 1681 and represented by the U-M/General Corpus HarLyr encoding. Its texts are Middle English except that Brook 28 / Ker 69 alternates Middle English and Anglo-Norman. It comprises Brook lyric numbers 1-18 and 20-32, not one authored medieval work, not every English lyric in Harley 2253, not Brook number 19, not all texts in the relevant folios or manuscript, not Brook's complete 1948 edition, not Ker's complete 1965 printed facsimile, and not the U-M or repository XML considered merely as digital artifacts.

catalog_summary.title: Harley Lyrics (selections)
catalog_summary.author: Anonymous lyric authors
catalog_summary.editor: Frances McSparran; transcriptions corrected by Meredith Jones; University of Virginia Electronic Text Center numbering and pagination follow G. L. Brook
catalog_summary.date: Lyrics individually composed before their first known attestation in a Harley 2253 component copied circa 1330-1340; exact digital selection deposited in 1992 and electronically published by U-M in 1993
catalog_summary.region: Selected witnesses copied in or near Ludlow in the West Midlands; individual lyric composition places remain unresolved

notes: The parenthetical boundary term comes from the OTA title and is retained because this is a thirty-one-item selection, not the complete set of Harley lyrics.
U-M and repository display title; it can suggest a broader boundary than the body actually encodes.

summaries.value: A thirty-one-item edited selection of anonymous Harley lyrics preserved in British Library MS Harley 2253. Middle English is primary, while Brook 28 / Ker 69 alternates Middle English and Anglo-Norman. Themes range across courtly love, seasonal nature, mortality, satire, penitence, Marian devotion, Christ's Passion, and salvation. The historical texts are heterogeneous and should not be assigned one author, composition date, or composition place.

The body has thirty-one verse divisions and 1,563 encoded lines. Division headings identify Brook lyrics 1-18 and 20-32 and Ker manuscript items 24b, 27-30, 33-36, 41, 43-46, 50-53, 60-67, 69, 73, 81, 92, and 93. Printed page milestones span Brook pp. 29-72.

Brook number 19 is deliberately absent. It is Ker item 55, ‘Dum ludis floribus,’ at Harley f. 76r: a twenty-line item with eighteen Latin/Anglo-Norman lines and two English closing lines. HarLyr excludes the whole multilingual item but retains Brook's subsequent numbering, so the gap defines an edited Middle-English-lyric selection rather than a lost XML division or an excerpted pair of English lines.

The exact OTA payload has 1,562 line elements; U-M has 1,563 because Ker item 46 gains one line and its following numbering is repaired. Eleven U-M replacement characters align with eleven legacy non-UTF-8 OTA bytes. The source history also says manuscript-joined words were segmented with a colon, for example ‘itelle’ as ‘i:telle.’ These are source-version and encoding-quality facts, not grounds for silent emendation. The U-M header says front matter, back matter, and printed editorial notes were omitted.

The lyric readings are new transcriptions made from Ker's 1965 EETS facsimile under McSparran's direction and corrected by Jones. Brook's 1948 edition supplies numbering, line references, and pagination only; HarLyr explicitly may differ from Brook. The 1965 volume is physically partial for Harley 2253 and Royal 12 C XII but complete for all HarLyr-selected witness passages. No lawful complete public online copy of that facsimile or current public Harley image set was found on 2026-07-14.

extent.description: The repository XML contains thirty-one top-level lyric divisions, thirty-one headings, and 1,563 L elements, with no LG or prose P elements in the body. Each division has continuous local line numbering. The numbered sequence follows Brook 1-18 and 20-32; the missing number 19 is an excluded complete multilingual poem, not an unencoded selected item. The exact OTA payload has 1,562 L-like elements because of one documented difference in Ker item 46. These are encoded-element counts, not a claim that every historical metrical line or stanza boundary is editorially settled.

content_parts.label: Thirty-one selected Harley lyrics
content_parts.summary: Thirty-one anonymous Middle English verse lyrics newly transcribed from Ker's 1965 facsimile and organized with Brook numbers 1-18 and 20-32. The items are heterogeneous in authorship, historical context, genre, and subject.

locators.value: Facsimile plates corresponding to the same selected Harley folios

notes: The manuscript folio surfaces include adjacent and intervening texts not selected by HarLyr.
Brook number 19 / Ker item 55 at f. 76r is outside this part even though its final two lines are English.
Ker's two Royal 12 C XII comparison folios have no target-work overlap and are not content parts.

evidence.locator: IDG, sourceDesc, editorial declarations, and complete BODY
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for title, displayed responsibilities, thirty-one selected lyrics, fresh facsimile transcription policy, University of Virginia addition of Brook-based numbering and pagination, omitted print matter, Middle English and Anglo-Norman content, and encoded themes.

evidence.citation: Namespace-free structural and byte-level audit of HarLyr.xml and exact OTA deposit files.
evidence.locator: BODY/DIV1, HEAD, L, PB, EPB; local line numbers; OTA div0/l comparison; replacement bytes
evidence.summary: Counts 31 DIV1, 31 HEAD, 1,563 L, 44 PB, and 45 EPB; verifies continuous local line numbering and the 1-18, 20-32 sequence; identifies eleven replacement characters; and localizes the one-line OTA/U-M difference to Ker item 46.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, ‘The Harley lyrics,’ HarLyr.
evidence.locator: Bibliographic display and thirty-one table-of-contents entries
evidence.summary: Official title, displayed editor data, 1965 and 1948 source dates, public text, and contents sequence 1-18 and 20-32.

evidence.citation: Oxford Text Archive, ‘Harley Lyrics (selections),’ deposit 1681, record, payload, and metadata header.
evidence.locator: Complete thirty-one-item payload and companion header
evidence.summary: Confirms the preferred selection title, 1992-04-03 deposit, student/McSparran/Jones transcription history, EETS facsimile source, exact item sequence, 1,562-line legacy structure, and public downloadable files.
notes: The companion header is separately cached with SHA-256 d1417217d5e5c205e63c739e2cf2b34a5a0a8573b20a02e113416aa76335382f.

evidence.citation: British Library, Archives and Manuscripts, Harley MS 2253 parent record and relevant second-part child record.
evidence.locator: Relevant part ff. 49r-142v; selected lyric item locators; date and production place
evidence.summary: Establishes the manuscript boundary, circa 1330-1340 date and near-Ludlow copying context, discontiguous selected-lyric folios, trilingual physical context, and current lack of public digital images.

evidence.citation: Eric Stanley, ‘What Six Unalike Lyrics in British Library MS Harley 2253 Have Alike in Manuscript Layout,’ chapter summary, together with METS article 55.
evidence.locator: Brook 19 / Ker item 55, f. 76r, complete twenty-line text
evidence.summary: Identifies the omitted item and confirms that its first eighteen lines are Latin/Anglo-Norman and its final two lines English, establishing a whole-item selection boundary.

evidence.citation: Susanna Fein, ed., with David Raybin and Jan Ziolkowski, The Complete Harley 2253 Manuscript, Middle English Texts Series, combined three-volume PDF.
evidence.locator: Complete 1,448-page scholarly edition and translation; corresponding Harley articles
evidence.summary: Later collateral evidence for individual texts, subject range, manuscript order, number-19 context, and the line present in METS item 46; not an upstream HarLyr source or photographic facsimile.

evidence.locator: entities, relations, access, rights, editorial practices, evidence, and open questions
evidence.summary: Companion graph establishing the repository/U-M/OTA version chain; facsimile rather than Brook transcription source; Harley and Royal coverage boundaries; access and cache facts; source-specific rights; and unresolved research issues.

open_questions.question: What composition dates, places, authorships, textual relationships, and item-level classifications can be established for each of the thirty-one lyrics?
next_steps: Research each Ker/BL item individually against current scholarship and witness catalogs.
Keep future assertions item-scoped rather than assigning one context to the edited selection.

open_questions.question: What exact characters should replace the eleven U+FFFD positions inherited from the OTA legacy bytes?
next_steps: Inspect an authorized copy of the 1965 facsimile at the affected lines.
Consult OTA encoding documentation before mapping the byte values.
Do not silently emend the source XML.

open_questions.question: Who made the U-M structural and textual revisions to OTA 1681, including the additional line in Ker item 46?
next_steps: Request conversion logs or correspondence from U-M and OTA.
Compare any surviving intermediate SGML and XML versions.

open_questions.question: Can a lawful complete digital copy of Ker's 1965 facsimile, or authorized images of all selected Harley folios, be obtained with clear reuse terms?
next_steps: Ask EETS, the British Library, and holding libraries about licensed access or reproduction orders.
Recheck the official BL records and major digitization catalogs periodically.

open_questions.question: How should U-M's current No Copyright-US display, OTA's CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 license, the embedded personal-use notice, and the CME Unlicense be reconciled for redistribution?
next_steps: Seek source-specific clarification from U-M, OTA, and the CME maintainer before commercial or unrestricted redistribution.
Keep medieval text, modern transcription, edition, image, and repository layers separate.

notes: The cataloged unit follows the exact intellectual selection represented by OTA 1681 and HarLyr, not the broadest title string or physical manuscript boundary.
Brook supplies identifiers and locators, not the transcribed readings; describing HarLyr as simply encoded from Brook would contradict the explicit source history.
The relevant Harley folio ranges contain adjacent unselected texts and should not be interpreted as exhaustive surface coverage.
Royal 12 C XII is modeled only because two ancillary leaves occur in Ker's facsimile; it transmits no HarLyr-selected content.
Witness date and copying place are not composition date and place. Individual lyric research remains deliberately open.

The legacy XML CREATION value ‘c. 1310’ is unsourced and is not adopted as a common composition date or witness date; the current British Library circa 1330-1340 date is scoped to the relevant manuscript part.
Rights, access, and cached-copy integrity are recorded in the companion lineage manifest and must not be inherited across medieval text, modern edition, facsimile, image, and encoding layers.
